findings.message: Snippet does not include the `experimental.plan: true` block present in the auto-generated Gemini settings produced by install.sh (lines 530-550). Manual applicants will miss the Gemini plan-mode enablement.

PreToolUse/EnterPlanMode hook calling `plannotator improve-context` (timeout: 5s) is present in hooks.json but absent from scripts/install.sh. Marketplace install gets this hook; install.sh users do not. The improve-context subcommand is also undocumented in the audited files.
Pattern `curl ... | bash` detected inside usage() heredoc (identical copy of scripts/install.sh)
`node -e` block with $GEMINI_SETTINGS interpolation — identical to scripts/install.sh finding; same risk and same fix applies

Astro content-collection docs page included in NL artifact scan. Has valid title/description frontmatter but carries no agent-instruction semantics. Scored as documentation type; well-written reference material.
Astro content-collection docs page included in NL artifact scan. Has valid title/description frontmatter but carries no agent-instruction semantics.
Astro content-collection docs page included in NL artifact scan. Has valid title/description frontmatter but carries no agent-instruction semantics.
Astro content-collection docs page included in NL artifact scan. Has valid title/description frontmatter but carries no agent-instruction semantics.

No branching for approved/dismissed/annotated decision states. Hook variant explicitly handles all three decision outcomes. OpenCode variant only acknowledges opening the UI without instructing the agent on any outcome.

File is an exact copy of the hook variant; contains !`plannotator archive` bang command and allowed-tools: Bash(plannotator:*) frontmatter that OpenCode does not process — archive subcommand is never invoked in OpenCode

File contains only a description field in frontmatter; the body is empty. Agent receives no instructions when the command is invoked. All other OpenCode commands (review, annotate, archive) have a brief instruction body.

No branching for empty output or approval state. Hook and copilot variants enumerate 3 explicit cases (approved, dismissed/empty, annotated). OpenCode variant only says 'wait for the user's feedback' with no guidance on empty or approve outcomes.
@anthropic-ai/claude-agent-sdk: ^0.2.92 — caret range permits minor/patch upgrades without explicit review
@openai/codex-sdk: ^0.118.0 — caret range
@opencode-ai/sdk: ^1.3.0 — caret range
@pierre/diffs: ^1.1.12 — caret range; shadow DOM internals make surprise minor-version breaks high-impact per pierre-guard skill
Pattern `curl -fsSL https://plannotator.ai/install.sh | bash` detected inside usage() heredoc at line 57
Pattern `curl -fsSL https://plannotator.ai/install.sh | bash -s -- --version vX.Y.Z` detected inside usage() heredoc at line 58
Pattern `curl -fsSL https://plannotator.ai/install.sh | bash -s -- --verify-attestation` detected inside usage() heredoc at line 59

`node -e` block at lines 508-514 embeds unquoted shell variables $GEMINI_SETTINGS and $PLANNOTATOR_HOOK directly into the JavaScript source string. If $HOME contains shell metacharacters or quotes the injected JavaScript is malformed. Both variables are OS-controlled or hardcoded so actual exploit surface is negligible, but the pattern is architecturally unsafe.

curl to GitHub API to resolve latest release tag: `curl -fsSL https://api.github.com/repos/$REPO/releases/latest`. Network call to external service on every install; result parsed via grep+cut without JSON validation.

curl downloads binary and SHA256 checksum from GitHub Releases. SHA256 is verified before install; SLSA attestation optionally verified via `gh attestation verify`. Download chain is intact and well-guarded.

`pi install npm:@plannotator/pi-extension` — installs npm package at runtime during install script execution, only when the `pi` CLI is detected. Pulls from npm registry without additional integrity check.

`git clone --depth 1 --sparse` from GitHub to install skills assets. Clones at `$latest_tag` which is resolved earlier in the script; tag is not pinned to a commit SHA so a force-pushed tag could serve different content.

File is an exact copy of the hook variant; contains !`plannotator archive` bang command and allowed-tools: Bash(plannotator:*) frontmatter that OpenCode does not process — archive subcommand is never invoked in OpenCode

File contains only a description field in frontmatter; the body is empty. Agent receives no instructions when the command is invoked. All other OpenCode commands (review, annotate, archive) have a brief instruction body.

No branching for empty output or approval state. Hook and copilot variants enumerate 3 explicit cases (approved, dismissed/empty, annotated). OpenCode variant only says 'wait for the user's feedback' with no guidance on empty or approve outcomes.

No branching for approved/dismissed/annotated decision states. Hook variant explicitly handles all three decision outcomes. OpenCode variant only acknowledges opening the UI without instructing the agent on any outcome.
